channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 20 Important Facts About Henry Nwosu, Nigerian Football Legend
item.description: The Nigerian football community dey mourn the loss of Henry Onyemanze Nwosu, one of the most talented midfielders wey Nigeria don ever get. Born on June 14, 1963, for Imo State, Nwosu rise to fame dur...

item.title: Nigerian Army stops armed attack while responding to communal dispute in Cross River
item.description: On Saturday, troops from the 13 Brigade of the Nigerian Army successfully defended themselves against a coordinated ambush by suspected armed criminals while responding to a communal dispute in Odonge...

item.title: Salman Ali Agha Receives ICC Reprimand After Bangladesh ODI Incident
item.description: Pakistan cricket captain Salman Ali Agha was officially reprimanded by the International Cricket Council (ICC) for breaching Level 1 of the Code of Conduct during the second ODI against Bangladesh in ...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte expresses concern over House request to take Madriaga into custody
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has expressed being disturbed by a recent request from Akbayan Representative Chel Diokno for the House of Representatives to place Ramil Madriaga under its custody. The ma...

item.title: Why Australian Farmers Purchase Large Diesel Stocks to Protect Food Supply
item.description: Over recent weeks, rising petrol and diesel prices have sparked concerns across Australia about potential impacts on food prices. While urban motorists feel the pinch, farmers in regional areas often ...

item.title: US Congressman Claims Virginia Shooter Had Links With ISIL In Nigeria Before Attack
item.description: US Congressman Riley Moore don raise alarm say the guy wey attack Old Dominion University for Virginia, Bailor Jalloh, bin meet with Islamic State of Iraq and Levant (ISIL) members for Nigeria before ...

item.title: How Artificial Intelligence Is Redefining Filmmaking in Hollywood
item.description: The article explores how artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming Hollywood as the film industry braces for the 2026 Oscars. The author, a professor at USC’s School of Cinematic Arts, highlight...

item.title: New mobile app launched in Nigeria to verify antibiotics and fight fake drugs
item.description: For Abuja, stakeholders wey dey inside Nigeria One Health sector don show face to launch one new mobile app wey dem call Community Access to Effective Antibiotics (COM-WATCH). The aim of the app na to...

item.title: Federal funding for air passenger complaint backlog may expire, increasing wait times
item.description: The federal government is reviewing whether to renew temporary funding aimed at reducing a growing backlog of airline passenger complaints handled by the Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA). Establis...

item.title: Bird Diversity and Wildlife of Rubondo Island National Park
item.description: Rubondo Island National Park, located in the southwest of Lake Victoria, Tanzania, is a special place for wildlife lovers, especially for bird watchers. Covering about 457 square kilometres, with the ...

item.title: Bihar Recognised for Significant Efforts in Sports Development
item.description: Bihar has been awarded the 'Best State for Promotion of Sports' at the Sportstar Aces Awards 2026, reflecting the State’s active commitment to creating a dynamic sporting environment. The award was pr...

item.title: DA Supports Minister Schreiber's Measures to Combat Corruption in Border Management
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has welcomed decisive action by Minister Schreiber against corruption in South Africa's immigration and border systems. Fifty officials from the Border Management Authorit...

item.title: Kenyan universities warned to urgently prepare for Competency-Based Education transition
item.description: This article argues that Kenyan universities are walking into an avoidable crisis by failing to adequately prepare for the full rollout of Competency-Based Education (CBE) and the transition of the fi...

item.title: Challenges and delays in the UK’s AI datacentre investment boom
item.description: The article examines growing concerns over the UK’s massive investments in AI datacentres, questioning whether an overhyped bubble is forming amid delays and unfulfilled promises. It highlights the St...

item.title: Reflections on Truthfulness, Virtue, and Relationship Challenges
item.description: The article recounts a personal story of moral conflict and self-deception within a long-distance romantic relationship. The author, a philosophy professor, had been with her boyfriend, Tyler, for alm...

item.title: Daily Mini Crossword and Puzzle Challenges
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald presents its latest Superquiz for Sunday, 15 March 2026, inviting readers to test their skills with a Mini Crossword. This bite-sized puzzle consists of ten clues spread over...

item.title: Government announces salary cuts and strict austerity plan amid global oil crisis
item.description: The federal government has announced that employees of state-owned enterprises (SOEs) and autonomous institutions under government supervision will face salary reductions ranging from five to thirty p...

item.title: Southern Africa to strengthen regional power networks and expand renewable energy
item.description: Leaders from across Southern Africa gathered in Lusaka for a high-level Southern African Power Pool (SAPP) meeting, highlighting the urgent need to modernise and expand the region’s electricity infras...

item.title: Raptors defeat Suns 122-115 with key contributions from Barnes and Barrett
item.description: The Toronto Raptors claimed a solid 122-115 victory against the Phoenix Suns in a high-scoring match that highlighted the growing synergy between young stars Scottie Barnes and OG Anunoby Barrett. Bot...

item.title: Waiguru Urges Gachagua to Engage Ruto Directly Amid Political Tensions in Mt Kenya
